title: Living in Boulder vlog 2 -  2 -  2018: Google Earthing Circus Maximus

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night vlog update from Boulder about his ongoing Google Earth historical reconstruction project, specifically his work modeling ancient Rome. He discusses finishing the Colosseum model, starting the Circus Maximus, using a YouTube walking-tour channel (Pro Walks) to trace the route in his model, and frustrations with Google Earth crashing and losing unsaved work.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Now more interested in Circus Maximus than Colosseum because it was much larger even though it's mostly gone
Google Earth is not as realistic as Sketchup but offers x-ray vision that works well for ruins
His version is better because it sits on the actual earth terrain rather than floating
Not modeling it with as much detail as the Colosseum — grouping stairs into blocks to save time

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: You can't delete videos on YouTube anymore, you can only private them.

claims.subject: YouTube
claims.claim: YouTube might have deleted videos to free up gigabytes of storage, but we just don't know about it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle had to private a video because the sound was screwed up on it.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle can't remember what he said in his last video.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is deciding whether to put stairs to the bottom part or the whole thing in his project.

claims.subject: YouTube channel
claims.claim: There's a YouTube channel called Pro Walks that Kyle recommends checking out.

claims.subject: Pro Walks
claims.claim: Pro Walks puts a GoPro on his hat and walks slowly around sparkly areas, making you feel like you're right there.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle traced a Pro Walks video of Rome using Google Earth and his own model.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Kyle's Google Earth download page includes Region three, which covers the city of Rome.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle worked on the Colosseum in detail, including every single stair.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Kyle is working on the Circus Maximus with less detail than the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Circus Maximus is designed to work simply in the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Circus Maximus had a capacity of 385,000 people.

claims.subject: Circus Maximus
claims.claim: Circus Maximus was like an ancient indoor mall with a big racetrack in the center.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle is more interested in the Circus Maximus now than the Colosseum.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle's Colosseum version is better because he's right on the earth, making it more realistic.

claims.subject: Google Earth
claims.claim: Kyle's Google Earth keeps crashing when he records with audio using QuickTime.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Kyle has to save his work frequently because Google Earth keeps crashing.
